summary: The round-2 approved posture remains sound. The new per-lane accounting preserves worker, review, fix, and disarm outcomes independently; hard lease/revalidation/workflow-launch failures are classified as errors, capacity holds remain deferred, and failed launches release their leases. The workflow health step treats any disarm error or wholly stalled review/fix lane as zero-dispatch even when another lane launches, while emitting only coarse counts. The self-tests are non-vacuous: they cover serialization and derived deferrals, exhaustive review-state routing, capacity-vs-error classification, real failed/successful workflow-run branches with lease-release assertions, and disarm error/defer/success accounting. No trust check, audit binding, privilege boundary, or sensitive-data handling is weakened, and no new correctness or security issue is supported by the diff.
